comment_text.sn35.233:1.2: Kāmabhū discusses Dhamma with the wise layman Citta at SN 41.5 and SN 41.6.

root_text.sn35.233:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca ānando āyasmā ca kāmabhū kosambiyaṁ viharanti ghositārāme.
root_text.sn35.233:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā kāmabhū sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn35.233:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā kāmabhū āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.233:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso ānanda, cakkhu rūpānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, rūpā cakkhussa saṁyojanaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.233:2.2: jivhā rasānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, rasā jivhāya saṁyojanaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.233:2.3: mano dhammānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, dhammā manassa saṁyojanan”ti?
root_text.sn35.233:3.1: “Na kho, āvuso kāmabhū, cakkhu rūpānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, na rūpā cakkhussa saṁyojanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.233:3.2: Yañca tattha tadubhayaṁ paṭicca uppajjati chandarāgo, taṁ tattha saṁyojanaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.233:3.3: na jivhā rasānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, na rasā jivhāya saṁyojanaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.233:3.4: na mano dhammānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, na dhammā manassa saṁyojanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.233:3.5: Yañca tattha tadubhayaṁ paṭicca uppajjati chandarāgo taṁ tattha saṁyojanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.233:4.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, kāḷo ca balībaddo odāto ca balībaddo ekena dāmena vā yottena vā saṁyuttā assu.
root_text.sn35.233:4.3: ‘kāḷo balībaddo odātassa balībaddassa saṁyojanaṁ, odāto balībaddo kāḷassa balībaddassa saṁyojanan’ti, sammā nu kho so vadamāno vadeyyā”ti?
root_text.sn35.233:4.5: “Na kho, āvuso, kāḷo balībaddo odātassa balībaddassa saṁyojanaṁ, napi odāto balībaddo kāḷassa balībaddassa saṁyojanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.233:4.6: Yena ca kho te ekena dāmena vā yottena vā saṁyuttā, taṁ tattha saṁyojanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.233:4.7: Evameva kho, āvuso, na cakkhu rūpānaṁ saṁyojanaṁ, na rūpā cakkhussa saṁyojanaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.233:4.10: yañca tattha tadubhayaṁ paṭicca uppajjati chandarāgo, taṁ tattha saṁyojanan”ti.

translation_text.sn35.233:1.1: At one time the venerables Ānanda and Kāmabhū were staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn35.233:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Kāmabhū came out of retreat, went to Venerable Ānanda, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn35.233:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Ānanda:
translation_text.sn35.233:2.1: “Reverend Ānanda, which is it? Is the eye the fetter of sights, or are sights the fetter of the eye?
translation_text.sn35.233:2.2: Is the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.233:2.3: mind the fetter of ideas, or are ideas the fetter of the mind?”
translation_text.sn35.233:3.1: “Reverend Kāmabhū, the eye is not the fetter of sights, nor are sights the fetter of the eye.
translation_text.sn35.233:3.2: The fetter there is the desire and greed that arises from the pair of them.
translation_text.sn35.233:3.4: mind is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the mind.
translation_text.sn35.233:3.5: The fetter there is the desire and greed that arises from the pair of them.
translation_text.sn35.233:4.1: Suppose there was a black ox and a white ox yoked by a single harness or yoke.
translation_text.sn35.233:4.2: Would it be right to say that
translation_text.sn35.233:4.3: the black ox is the yoke of the white ox, or the white ox is the yoke of the black ox?”
translation_text.sn35.233:4.5: The black ox is not the yoke of the white ox, nor is the white ox the yoke of the black ox.
translation_text.sn35.233:4.6: The yoke there is the single harness or yoke that they’re yoked by.”
translation_text.sn35.233:4.7: “In the same way, the eye is not the fetter of sights, nor are sights the fetter of the eye.
translation_text.sn35.233:4.9: mind is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the mind.
translation_text.sn35.233:4.10: The fetter there is the desire and greed that arises from the pair of them.”
